definition: Participant ID from the participant_id column, manual map, or sub-<id> in the filename.
Session label from the session column, manual map, or ses-<id> in the filename.
Source CSV filename for this run.
Full path to the source CSV.
MD5 hash of the file; identical hashes flag duplicate runs.
True if another analyzed file has the same MD5 hash.
Number of analyzed files sharing this MD5 hash.
TASK_VERSION stamped in the data rows (provenance).
RNG seed for the run; reproduces the bandit schedule.
Food category shown this session: sweet, savory, or sweet+savory (between-subjects).
Bandit rows present in the file.
Bandit trials with a recorded choice and a valid reward/loss outcome.
Proportion of bandit rows without a usable choice/outcome.
Highest swap_count reached; equals the number of reversals completed.
Proportion of usable bandit trials choosing the current highest-probability arm.
Proportion of usable bandit trials that were rewarded.
Sum of bandit points across usable trials.
Mean of optimal_points minus obtained points across usable trials.
Mean choice reaction time (s); the primary implicit wanting/vigor signal in the bandit.
Median choice reaction time (s); used as the general-speed covariate for residualized vigor.
Proportion of choice RTs below 0.15 s.
Proportion of choice RTs below 0.1 s.
Proportion of bandit trials where the 4 s 'answer faster' nudge fired (choice_late).
Optimal-choice accuracy over the first 15 usable trials of each phase, pooled.
Optimal-choice accuracy over the last 15 usable trials of each phase, pooled.
Optimal-choice accuracy over the last 15 usable trials of the final phase; basis for the asymptote QC flag.
Proportion of consecutive usable trials with a different position choice (exploration index).
Normalized Shannon entropy (0-1) of position choices across usable trials.
Proportion of rewarded usable trials whose next usable trial repeats the same symbol.
Proportion of non-rewarded usable trials whose next usable trial switches symbol.
Rewarded consecutive-trial transitions available for win-stay.
Non-rewarded consecutive-trial transitions available for lose-shift.
Optimal-choice accuracy in the 10 trials before reversal 1.
Optimal-choice accuracy in the 10 trials after reversal 1.
Proportion of post-reversal-1 choices on the position that was optimal just before reversal 1.
rev1_pre_optimal minus rev1_post_optimal (reversal accuracy drop).
Optimal-choice accuracy in the 10 trials before reversal 2.
Optimal-choice accuracy in the 10 trials after reversal 2.
Proportion of post-reversal-2 choices on the position that was optimal just before reversal 2.
rev2_pre_optimal minus rev2_post_optimal.
Mean perseveration across both reversals.
Mean post-reversal optimal-choice accuracy across both reversals.
Mean pre-reversal accuracy minus mean post-reversal accuracy across both reversals.
Mini-MID probe rows present (expected 30: 16 food + 14 neutral).
Food-cue probe rows.
Neutral-cue probe rows.
Proportion of probes with a target hit; the staircase targets about 0.667.
Hit rate on food-cue probes.
Hit rate on neutral-cue probes.
Proportion of probes with a too-soon (anticipatory) press.
Proportion of probes with no press within the grace window.
Mean target RT (ms) on food-cue probes; raw food-cued vigor signal.
Median target RT (ms) on food-cue probes.
Mean target RT (ms) on neutral-cue probes.
Median target RT (ms) on neutral-cue probes.
Food probes with a valid target RT (used for vigor eligibility).
Neutral probes with a valid target RT.
Food minus neutral mean target RT; an individual-difference contrast, NOT a within-subject manipulation (no control trials).
Total mini-MID bonus points earned.
Adaptive response window (ms) on the last probe; basis for floor/ceiling QC flags.
Mean adaptive response window (ms) across probes.
Mean bandit choice RT (s) on the usable trial immediately after each food probe.
Mean bandit choice RT (s) on the usable trial immediately after each neutral probe.
post_food_choice_rt minus post_neutral_choice_rt.
Optimal-choice rate on the usable trial immediately after each food probe.
Optimal-choice rate on the usable trial immediately after each neutral probe.
Usable post-food-probe bandit trials contributing to post-food measures.
Usable post-neutral-probe bandit trials contributing to post-neutral measures.
Win-stay on the post-food-probe trial: repeat after a rewarded post-probe trial. Few transitions; interpret with care.
Lose-shift on the post-food-probe trial: switch after a non-rewarded post-probe trial. Few transitions.
Rewarded post-food-probe transitions available for post_food_win_stay.
Non-rewarded post-food-probe transitions available for post_food_lose_shift.
Across-subject -z of food_mid_mean_rt_ms (higher = faster food responding = more vigor).
Across-subject -z of food_mid_median_rt_ms.
PRIMARY hybrid vigor index: food RT residualized on bandit median RT, MID no-response, and premature rates, sign-flipped and z-scored across subjects.
Semicolon-separated QC flags from the USER SETTINGS thresholds.
Number of QC flags for this file.
True if a flag compromises interpretability (few trials, near-random asymptote, broken MID staircase, or excessive anticipation).
Human-readable reasons behind recommended_exclusion.
bandit_phase_summary / trial export: 1-indexed reversal phase (phase 1 = before any reversal).
bandit_phase_summary: usable bandit trials in the phase.
bandit_phase_summary: optimal-choice accuracy within the phase.
bandit_phase_summary: reward rate within the phase.
bandit_phase_summary: mean choice RT (s) within the phase.
mid_cue_summary: food or neutral.
mid_cue_summary: probes of this cue type.
mid_cue_summary: hit rate for this cue type.
mid_cue_summary: mean target RT (ms) for this cue type.
mid_cue_summary: median target RT (ms) for this cue type.
